info.title: Ishraf Platform Backend API
info.description: Full OpenAPI coverage for all live backend endpoints. /health and /health/ready are documented against the service root, while the rest use /api/v1.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Service health and readiness endpoints mounted outside /api/v1.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Authentication, session lifecycle, password recovery, and current-user endpoints.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Admin-only user management endpoints for all roles.

tags.name: Academic Structure
tags.description: Admin-only academic years, semesters, grade levels, classes, subjects, and staff assignment endpoints.

tags.name: Students
tags.description: Admin-only student lifecycle, parent linking, and promotion endpoints.

tags.name: System Settings
tags.description: Admin-only global feature flags, settings audit, and integration outbox summary endpoints.

tags.name: Attendance
tags.description: Session-based attendance endpoints used by admin, teachers, and supervisors.

tags.name: Assessments
tags.description: Assessment types, assessments, and student score roster endpoints.

tags.name: Behavior
tags.description: Behavior categories, behavior records, and student behavior timelines.

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: AI analytics jobs, snapshot publishing/review, feedback, and read-only analytics summary endpoints.

tags.name: Transport
tags.description: Transport static data, assignments, trips, live locations, and trip student events.

tags.name: Communication
tags.description: Direct messages, announcements, and notification center endpoints.

tags.name: Admin Imports
tags.description: Admin-only school onboarding import endpoints for dry-run, apply, and import audit history.

tags.name: Homework
tags.description: Homework management, submission rosters, and student homework surfaces.

tags.name: Reporting
tags.description: Role dashboards, student-scoped reports, transport summaries, and parent live-status endpoints.

CreateUserRequest.description: Discriminated by role. profile is role-specific: parent(address, relationType), teacher(specialization, qualification, hireDate), supervisor(department), driver(licenseNumber, driverStatus), admin(null or omitted).

teacherId.description: Admin flows must send teacherId. Teacher flows must omit this field and let the backend derive it from the authenticated teacher.

academicYearId.description: Optional operational field. If omitted, the backend resolves the active academic year. If sent, it must match the active academic year.

semesterId.description: Optional operational field. If omitted, the backend resolves the active semester. If sent, it must match the active semester.

academicYearId.description: Optional operational field. If omitted, the backend resolves the active academic year. If sent, it must match the active academic year.

semesterId.description: Optional operational field. If omitted, the backend resolves the active semester. If sent, it must match the active semester.

teacherId.description: Admin flows must send teacherId. Teacher flows must omit this field and let the backend derive it from the authenticated teacher.

records.description: Full roster snapshot. Every active student in the session roster must appear exactly once.

teacherId.description: Admin flows must send teacherId. Teacher flows must omit this field and let the backend derive it from the authenticated teacher.

academicYearId.description: Optional operational field. If omitted, the backend resolves the active academic year. If sent, it must match the active academic year.

semesterId.description: Optional operational field. If omitted, the backend resolves the active semester. If sent, it must match the active semester.

workbook.description: Structured workbook JSON. The backend does not accept raw Excel binary upload in v1.

dryRunId.description: Must reference a validated dry-run result.

example.reason: Enable derived ETA between provider refreshes
Enable analytics runtime with scheduled dispatch

example.description: Selects the preferred ETA provider. Batch 2 activates runtime provider resolution with mapbox as the default selected provider.
Feature flags and provider selection for external map and ETA integrations used by transport surfaces.

entries.description: Selects the preferred ETA provider. Batch 2 activates runtime provider resolution with mapbox as the default selected provider.
Allows the backend worker to derive ETA snapshots locally between provider refreshes using the cached polyline and recent trip locations.
Keeps Google Maps ETA execution available when the admin explicitly selects Google as the active provider.
Minimum seconds between provider refreshes for one active trip ETA snapshot.
Route deviation threshold in meters that forces a fresh provider ETA snapshot.

groups.description: Feature flags that gate future FCM and realtime push delivery behavior.

entries.description: Enables Firebase Cloud Messaging provider usage when the provider phase is implemented.
Allows transport realtime notification workflows to publish through the push pipeline.

groups.description: Feature flags and provider selection for external map and ETA integrations used by transport surfaces.

entries.description: Selects the preferred ETA provider. Batch 2 activates runtime provider resolution with mapbox as the default selected provider.
Allows the backend worker to derive ETA snapshots locally between provider refreshes using the cached polyline and recent trip locations.
Keeps Google Maps ETA execution available when the admin explicitly selects Google as the active provider.
Minimum seconds between provider refreshes for one active trip ETA snapshot.
Route deviation threshold in meters that forces a fresh provider ETA snapshot.

groups.description: Feature flags, provider selection, and admin-controlled scheduled orchestration for AI analytics snapshots.

entries.description: Enables analytics job execution and AI-assisted insight generation for admin-triggered snapshots.
Selects the primary AI provider used to interpret deterministic analytics features into narrative insights.
Selects the fallback AI provider used only when the primary provider cannot complete the analytics interpretation.
Allows admin-controlled scheduled recompute dispatch cycles to create stale analytics jobs from the configured target list.
Defines the freshness window in minutes; scheduled dispatch only recreates analytics when no snapshot exists or the latest snapshot is older than this interval.
Caps how many stale subjects each scheduled dispatch cycle may enqueue per analytics target.
Defines which analytics targets the scheduled dispatch cycle is allowed to recompute when stale.
Allows the autonomous analytics scheduler worker to trigger stale scheduled-dispatch cycles without an interactive dashboard request.
Defines the active admin user id that will own autonomous scheduled analytics jobs for audit traceability.
Allows admin-triggered analytics retention cleanup to purge obsolete snapshots and terminal analytics execution records.
Defines how long obsolete analytics snapshots remain before cleanup deletes draft, rejected, and superseded snapshots. Approved snapshots remain available until they become superseded or rejected.
Defines how long completed, failed, and dead analytics jobs are retained before cleanup removes them.
Defines how long completed or failed analytics scheduler run records remain before cleanup removes them.

groups.description: Operational switches for admin-managed import capabilities.

entries.description: Enables the structured school onboarding dry-run/apply workflow.
Enables future CSV import surfaces when that operational flow is introduced.

get.description:
Return liveness information for the deployed service.

Public endpoint.

Public endpoint.

Change the current authenticated user password.

Allowed roles: admin, parent, teacher, supervisor, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Request a password reset token for an active account. Staging usually does not expose resetToken in the response.

Public endpoint.

Authenticate with email or phone and issue access and refresh tokens.

Public endpoint.

Revoke the provided refresh token.

Public endpoint.

post.description:
Exchange a valid refresh token for a fresh access/refresh pair.

Public endpoint.

Reset the password using a valid reset token. Successful reset revokes existing refresh tokens.

Public endpoint.

get.description:
Return the active academic year and active semester used by operational admin surfaces.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] This is the canonical global context for daily admin operations.

Note: If either the active academic year or active semester is missing, operational endpoints may return 409 Academic context not configured.

Atomically set the active academic year and active semester for operational surfaces.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] Use this as the canonical activation flow instead of activating the academic year and semester separately.

Note: The selected semester must belong to the selected academic year.

get.description:
List supervisor assignments with optional academic year, class, and supervisor filters.

Allowed roles: admin.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: supervisorId
parameters.description: Filter by supervisor identifier. Accepts the supervisor user id from /users?role=supervisor or the legacy supervisor profile id.

post.description:
Assign a supervisor to a class for an academic year. supervisorId accepts the supervisor user id from /users?role=supervisor or the legacy supervisor profile id.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: Frontend should send the supervisor user id returned by GET /users?role=supervisor. The backend resolves it internally to the stored supervisor profile id.

Note: Legacy integrations may still send the supervisor profile id directly.

Note: If the same value matches a supervisor user id and a different supervisor profile id, the backend rejects the request with SUPERVISOR_ID_AMBIGUOUS instead of guessing.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

Return one supervisor assignment by id.

Allowed roles: admin.

patch.description:
Update a supervisor assignment for administrative corrections. supervisorId accepts the supervisor user id from /users?role=supervisor or the legacy supervisor profile id.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] Supervisor assignment updates preserve the existing compatibility and supervisor-resolution rules.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

List teacher assignments with optional academic year, class, subject, and teacher filters.

Allowed roles: admin.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

Assign a teacher to a class and subject for an academic year. teacherId accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: Frontend should send the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher. The backend resolves it internally to the stored teacher profile id.

Note: Legacy integrations may still send the teacher profile id directly.

Note: If the same value matches a teacher user id and a different teacher profile id, the backend rejects the request with TEACHER_ID_AMBIGUOUS instead of guessing.

Return parent links for one student.

Allowed roles: admin.

Link an existing parent to a student. parentId may be either the parent user id returned by /users?role=parent or the underlying parent profile id.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: The backend resolves parentId to the stored parent profile id automatically. Prefer sending the user id returned by /users?role=parent in admin frontend flows.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Promote a student to another class for a target academic year while writing structured enrollment state and promotion history.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: The promotion writes the target academic enrollment even when the target year is not active yet.

Note: students.class_id is only synchronized immediately when the target year is the active academic year.

patch.description:
Mark one linked parent as the primary parent for that student. parentId may be either the parent user id returned by /users?role=parent or the underlying parent profile id.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: The backend resolves parentId to the stored parent profile id automatically. Prefer sending the user id returned by /users?role=parent in admin frontend flows.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: studentId
parameters.description: Student numeric string identifier. This is the same id used by /students/:id.

parameters.name: parentId
parameters.description: Parent identifier. These student-parent endpoints accept either the parent user id from /users?role=parent or the underlying parent profile id.

get.description:
Return every implemented system settings group with effective values merged from code defaults and stored overrides.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] Step 1 is global-only and currently implements pushNotifications, transportMaps, analytics, and imports.

Note: [NEW] Each entry returns the effective value, the code default, and whether the source is default or override.

Note: [NEW] transportMaps.etaProvider defaults to mapbox and is applied by runtime provider resolution.

Note: [NEW] transportMaps.etaDerivedEstimateEnabled defaults to true and controls local ETA math between provider refreshes.

Keeps Google Maps ETA execution available when the admin explicitly selects Google as the active provider.

Route deviation threshold in meters that forces a fresh provider ETA snapshot.

Defines the freshness window in minutes; scheduled dispatch only recreates analytics when no snapshot exists or the latest snapshot is older than this interval.

Allows the autonomous analytics scheduler worker to trigger stale scheduled-dispatch cycles without an interactive dashboard request.
Defines the active admin user id that will own autonomous scheduled analytics jobs for audit traceability.
Allows admin-triggered analytics retention cleanup to purge obsolete snapshots and terminal analytics execution records.
Defines how long obsolete analytics snapshots remain before cleanup deletes draft, rejected, and superseded snapshots. Approved snapshots remain available until they become superseded or rejected.
Defines how long completed, failed, and dead analytics jobs are retained before cleanup removes them.
Defines how long completed or failed analytics scheduler run records remain before cleanup removes them.

entries.description: Enables the structured school onboarding dry-run/apply workflow.
Enables future CSV import surfaces when that operational flow is introduced.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
Return one system settings group with effective values, defaults, descriptions, and override metadata.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] Allowed groups are pushNotifications, transportMaps, analytics, and imports.

Note: [NEW] transportMaps.etaProvider is part of the admin control plane and defaults to mapbox.

example.message: [NEW] Get System Settings Group completed successfully

data.description: Feature flags and provider selection for external map and ETA integrations used by transport surfaces.

entries.description: Selects the preferred ETA provider. Batch 2 activates runtime provider resolution with mapbox as the default selected provider.
Allows the backend worker to derive ETA snapshots locally between provider refreshes using the cached polyline and recent trip locations.
Keeps Google Maps ETA execution available when the admin explicitly selects Google as the active provider.
Minimum seconds between provider refreshes for one active trip ETA snapshot.
Route deviation threshold in meters that forces a fresh provider ETA snapshot.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: System settings group key. Allowed values: pushNotifications, transportMaps, analytics, imports.

patch.description:
Update one system settings group by writing overrides only for values that differ from code defaults.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] The request body is group-specific. Use UpdatePushNotificationsSettingsRequest, UpdateTransportMapsSettingsRequest, UpdateAnalyticsSettingsRequest, or UpdateImportsSettingsRequest depending on :group.

Note: [NEW] Sending a value equal to the code default clears any existing override instead of storing redundant data.

Note: [NEW] transportMaps updates runtime behavior through etaProvider and etaDerivedEstimateEnabled.

Note: [NEW] The first live consumer is imports.schoolOnboardingEnabled. When it is false, school onboarding import endpoints reject with 409 FEATURE_DISABLED.

example.message: [NEW] Update System Settings Group completed successfully

data.description: Feature flags and provider selection for external map and ETA integrations used by transport surfaces.

entries.description: Selects the preferred ETA provider. Batch 2 activates runtime provider resolution with mapbox as the default selected provider.
Allows the backend worker to derive ETA snapshots locally between provider refreshes using the cached polyline and recent trip locations.
Keeps Google Maps ETA execution available when the admin explicitly selects Google as the active provider.
Minimum seconds between provider refreshes for one active trip ETA snapshot.
Route deviation threshold in meters that forces a fresh provider ETA snapshot.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: System settings group key. Allowed values: pushNotifications, transportMaps, analytics, imports.

get.description:
List append-only audit rows for setting changes with pagination and optional filters.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] Audit rows capture created, updated, and cleared actions.

Note: [NEW] requestId lets admin trace one change batch across multiple keys.

example.message: [NEW] List System Settings Audit completed successfully

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Filter audit rows by settings group.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Filter audit rows by setting key.

parameters.name: changedByUserId
parameters.description: Filter audit rows by actor user id.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Filter audit rows created on or after this ISO datetime.

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Filter audit rows created on or before this ISO datetime.

get.description:
Return feature-enabled status for external integration groups together with pending and failed integration outbox counts.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] This surface only covers pushNotifications, transportMaps, and analytics.

Note: [NEW] providerConfigured is intentionally not returned; this surface tracks feature state and integration outbox workload only.

example.message: [NEW] Get Integration Feature Status completed successfully

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

patch.description:
Update one attendance record status or notes.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

Side effects: If the record changes into absent, the internal automation service may create notifications for linked parents.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
List attendance sessions for the active academic context with pagination. There is no root /attendance endpoint.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

Note: Empty collections return 200 with items=[] and pagination metadata.

Note: This operational list is scoped to the active academic year and active semester.

Note: teacherId filter accepts the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id. Prefer the user id in admin frontend flows.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: teacherId
parameters.description: Filter by teacher identifier. Accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

post.description:
Create an attendance session inside the active academic context. Admin teacherId accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id. Teacher flows must omit teacherId.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

Note: academicYearId and semesterId may be omitted; the backend resolves the active academic context automatically.

Note: If academicYearId or semesterId is sent, it must match the active context.

Note: The selected subjectId must have an active subject offering for the resolved semester.

Note: Admin frontend flows should send teacherId as the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher.

Note: Relevant domain errors include CLASS_YEAR_MISMATCH, SEMESTER_YEAR_MISMATCH, SUBJECT_GRADE_LEVEL_MISMATCH, SUBJECT_NOT_OFFERED_IN_SEMESTER, TEACHER_ID_REQUIRED, and ACADEMIC_CONTEXT_NOT_CONFIGURED.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
Return one attendance session including its student roster.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

put.description:
Save the full attendance roster snapshot for one session.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

Note: This payload is a full snapshot: every active student in the session roster must appear exactly once.

Note: Relevant domain errors include ATTENDANCE_DUPLICATE_STUDENT, ATTENDANCE_ROSTER_STUDENT_MISSING, and ATTENDANCE_ROSTER_STUDENT_NOT_ALLOWED.

Side effects: Any newly absent record can trigger parent notifications through the internal automation service.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
List assessments for the active academic context with pagination and filters.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

Note: This operational list is scoped to the active academic year and active semester.

Note: teacherId filter accepts the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id. Prefer the user id in admin frontend flows.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: teacherId
parameters.description: Filter by teacher identifier. Accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

post.description:
Create an assessment inside the active academic context. Teachers must omit teacherId and rely on the authenticated teacher profile. Admin teacherId accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

Note: academicYearId and semesterId may be omitted; the backend resolves the active academic context automatically.

Note: If academicYearId or semesterId is sent, it must match the active context.

Note: The selected subjectId must have an active subject offering for the resolved semester.

Note: Admin frontend flows should send teacherId as the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher.

Note: Teacher frontend flows must not send teacherId; the backend returns TEACHER_ID_NOT_ALLOWED if it is present.

Note: Relevant domain errors include CLASS_YEAR_MISMATCH, SEMESTER_YEAR_MISMATCH, SUBJECT_GRADE_LEVEL_MISMATCH, SUBJECT_NOT_OFFERED_IN_SEMESTER, TEACHER_ID_REQUIRED, TEACHER_ID_NOT_ALLOWED, and ACADEMIC_CONTEXT_NOT_CONFIGURED.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
Return one assessment detail record.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
Return the score roster for one assessment.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

put.description:
Create or update score rows for one assessment roster.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

Note: The payload does not need to include every student in the roster.

Note: Every submitted studentId must belong to the assessment roster.

Note: Relevant domain errors include ASSESSMENT_SCORE_EXCEEDS_MAX_SCORE, STUDENT_ASSESSMENT_DUPLICATE_STUDENT, and STUDENT_ASSESSMENT_STUDENT_NOT_ALLOWED.

Note: Runtime provider selection is active through transportMaps.etaProvider with mapbox as the default selected provider.

Note: ETA is a backend snapshot surface; live GPS streaming is read directly from Firebase RTDB after realtime token bootstrap.

Derived data: The response is built from transport_trip_eta_snapshots, transport_trip_eta_stop_snapshots, and the cached route polyline when available.

post.description:
Create one trip student event. stopId is required for boarded and dropped_off events, and must be omitted for absent.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

Note: Trip student event validation is trip-date aware: the student must have a transport assignment covering the trip date and route.

Note: Events are only accepted while the trip is started or ended. Invalid state returns TRIP_EVENT_STATUS_INVALID.

Note: A mismatching route assignment or stop produces STUDENT_TRIP_DATE_ASSIGNMENT_NOT_FOUND, TRIP_STUDENT_ROUTE_MISMATCH, or TRIP_EVENT_STOP_ROUTE_MISMATCH.

Note: All trip student events can enqueue FCM wake-up events when pushNotifications.fcmEnabled and pushNotifications.transportRealtimeEnabled are both true. dropped_off also writes an in-app parent notification.

Side effects: Trip student events write PostgreSQL truth first, then emit async automation work through integration_outbox.

get.description:
List homework for the active academic context with pagination and academic filters.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

Note: This operational list is scoped to the active academic year and active semester.

Note: teacherId filter accepts the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id. Prefer the user id in admin frontend flows.

Derived data: Homework lists are enriched by SQL views such as vw_homework_details.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: teacherId
parameters.description: Filter by teacher identifier. Accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

post.description:
Create homework inside the active academic context. Teachers must omit teacherId and rely on the authenticated teacher profile. Admin teacherId accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

Note: academicYearId and semesterId may be omitted; the backend resolves the active academic context automatically.

Note: If academicYearId or semesterId is sent, it must match the active context.

Note: The selected subjectId must have an active subject offering for the resolved semester.

Note: Admin frontend flows should send teacherId as the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher.

Note: Teacher frontend flows must not send teacherId; the backend returns TEACHER_ID_NOT_ALLOWED if it is present.

Note: Relevant domain errors include CLASS_YEAR_MISMATCH, SEMESTER_YEAR_MISMATCH, SUBJECT_GRADE_LEVEL_MISMATCH, SUBJECT_NOT_OFFERED_IN_SEMESTER, TEACHER_ID_REQUIRED, TEACHER_ID_NOT_ALLOWED, and ACADEMIC_CONTEXT_NOT_CONFIGURED.
